workflow.name: Analyze public consultation data and coordinate governance with Claude AI
workflow.description:
## How It Works
This workflow automates environmental, social, and governance (ESG) data collection, compliance validation, and sustainability reporting for corporations managing complex regulatory requirements and stakeholder transparency expectations. Designed for sustainability officers, compliance teams, and investor relations departments, it solves the challenge of aggregating ESG metrics across global operations, validating data accuracy, and generating standardized reports for multiple frameworks. The system schedules regular monitoring, fetches consolidated ESG data from operational systems, generates S&D (sustainability and disclosure) submissions, validates compliance through dual AI agents (Compliance Analyzer ensures regulatory adherence, Decision Coordination orchestrates specialized sub-agents for aggregate analysis, traceability monitoring, summary generation, and governance reporting), checks star ratings for data quality, routes findings by compliance status (critical/routine), and produces standardized reports with traceability records. Organizations achieve 90% reduction in reporting cycle time, ensure multi-framework compliance, eliminate manual data aggregation errors, and maintain complete audit trails for regulatory scrutiny.
 
## Setup Steps
1. Connect **Schedule Trigger** for monitoring frequency 
2. Configure **ESG data sources** with API credentials
3. Add **AI model API keys** to Compliance Analyzer and Decision Coordination Agent nodes
4. Define **reporting frameworks** and compliance requirements in agent prompts
5. Set **quality rating thresholds** for data completeness and materiality scoring parameters
6. Configure **alert mechanisms** for critical compliance gaps requiring immediate remediation

## Prerequisites
ESG data management system access, AI service accounts
## Use Cases
Carbon emissions tracking and reporting, supply chain sustainability monitoring
## Customization
Modify agent prompts for industry-specific materiality topics
## Benefits
Reduces reporting cycle time by 90%, ensures multi-framework compliance simultaneously

workflow.name: Smart Public Consultation Analysis and Governance Coordination System

parameters.jsonSchemaExample:
{
  "submissionId": "string",
  "sentiment": "positive | neutral | negative | mixed",
  "keyThemes": ["string"],
  "stakeholderType": "individual | organization | government | advocacy_group",
  "urgencyLevel": "low | medium | high | critical",
  "policyRelevance": "number between 0 and 1",
  "evidenceQuality": "low | medium | high",
  "geographicScope": "local | regional | national | international",
  "impactedPopulation": "string",
  "recommendations": ["string"],
  "concerns": ["string"],
  "supportingData": "boolean",
  "conflictingViews": "boolean"
}

options.systemMessage:
=You are a Consultation Analysis Agent specialized in evaluating public feedback and consultation submissions.

Your task is to:
1. Analyze the structured submission content for sentiment, themes, and stakeholder perspectives
2. Identify key policy-relevant insights without making policy recommendations
3. Assess the quality and credibility of evidence provided
4. Categorize stakeholder type and geographic scope
5. Evaluate urgency and potential impact on affected populations
6. Extract specific concerns and recommendations made by the submitter
7. Identify any conflicting views or supporting data
8. Rate policy relevance on a scale of 0 to 1

IMPORTANT CONSTRAINTS:
- You analyze and categorize feedback objectively
- You DO NOT make policy recommendations
- You DO NOT evaluate the merit of policy positions
- You DO NOT influence substantive policy outcomes
- You focus on extracting, structuring, and categorizing information

Submission metadata available:
- Submission ID: {{ $json.submissionId || "N/A" }}
- Submitter type: {{ $json.submitterType || "N/A" }}
- Submission date: {{ $json.submissionDate || "N/A" }}
- Geographic location: {{ $json.location || "N/A" }}

Return your analysis in the structured format defined by the output parser.

parameters.description: Detects potential bias in consultation analysis including demographic bias, geographic bias, stakeholder representation bias, and temporal bias
Creates comprehensive traceability records for audit trails including analysis metadata, decision points, data lineage, and governance actions
Generates executive summaries, thematic summaries, and statistical summaries of consultation analysis for policymakers

parameters.jsonSchemaExample:
{
  "governanceDecision": "proceed | review_required | escalate | hold",
  "escalationLevel": "routine | standard | critical",
  "escalationReason": "string",
  "biasDetected": "boolean",
  "biasScore": "number between 0 and 1",
  "biasDetails": ["string"],
  "summaryGenerated": "boolean",
  "traceabilityRecordCreated": "boolean",
  "recommendedActions": ["string"],
  "stakeholdersToNotify": ["string"],
  "timelineForResponse": "immediate | 24_hours | 7_days | 30_days",
  "confidenceScore": "number between 0 and 1",
  "governanceNotes": "string"
}

options.systemMessage:
=You are a Decision Governance Agent responsible for coordinating consultation analysis governance without influencing substantive policy outcomes.

Your task is to:
1. Review aggregated consultation analysis results
2. Coordinate bias detection checks using the Bias Detection Tool
3. Generate executive summaries using the Summary Generation Tool
4. Create traceability records using the Traceability Record Tool
5. Determine appropriate escalation levels based on urgency, bias, and impact
6. Identify stakeholders who need to be notified
7. Recommend governance actions (NOT policy actions)
8. Ensure transparency and accountability in the consultation process

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS:
- You coordinate governance processes ONLY
- You DO NOT make policy recommendations
- You DO NOT evaluate the merit of policy positions
- You DO NOT influence substantive policy outcomes
- You focus on process integrity, transparency, and appropriate escalation

Escalation criteria:
- CRITICAL: Bias score > 0.7, or >50 critical urgency submissions, or significant stakeholder representation issues
- STANDARD: Bias score 0.4-0.7, or 20-50 high urgency submissions, or moderate concerns
- ROUTINE: Bias score < 0.4, standard consultation processing

Workflow:
1. First, call the Bias Detection Tool with the aggregated analysis data
2. Then, call the Summary Generation Tool with the aggregated analysis data
3. Then, call the Traceability Record Tool with governance metadata
4. Finally, determine escalation level and recommended governance actions

Configuration thresholds:
- Bias threshold: {{ $('Workflow Configuration').first().json.biasThreshold }}
- Critical escalation threshold: {{ $('Workflow Configuration').first().json.criticalEscalationThreshold }}
- Standard report threshold: {{ $('Workflow Configuration').first().json.standardReportThreshold }}

Return your governance decision in the structured format defined by the output parser.

parameters.content:
## Automated Data Collection & Dual-Agent Validation
**What**: Compliance Analyzer validates regulatory requirements; Decision Agent coordinates specialized ESG sub-agents  
**Why**: Parallel compliance checking and data quality analysis ensures accuracy across multiple reporting frameworks

## Prerequisites
ESG data management system access, AI service accounts
## Use Cases
Carbon emissions tracking and reporting, supply chain sustainability monitoring
## Customization
Modify agent prompts for industry-specific materiality topics
## Benefits
Reduces reporting cycle time by 90%, ensures multi-framework compliance simultaneously

## Setup Steps
1. Connect **Schedule Trigger** for monitoring frequency 
2. Configure **ESG data sources** with API credentials
3. Add **AI model API keys** to Compliance Analyzer and Decision Coordination Agent nodes
4. Define **reporting frameworks** and compliance requirements in agent prompts
5. Set **quality rating thresholds** for data completeness and materiality scoring parameters
6. Configure **alert mechanisms** for critical compliance gaps requiring immediate remediation

## How It Works
This workflow automates environmental, social, and governance (ESG) data collection, compliance validation, and sustainability reporting for corporations managing complex regulatory requirements and stakeholder transparency expectations. Designed for sustainability officers, compliance teams, and investor relations departments, it solves the challenge of aggregating ESG metrics across global operations, validating data accuracy, and generating standardized reports for multiple frameworks. The system schedules regular monitoring, fetches consolidated ESG data from operational systems, generates S&D (sustainability and disclosure) submissions, validates compliance through dual AI agents (Compliance Analyzer ensures regulatory adherence, Decision Coordination orchestrates specialized sub-agents for aggregate analysis, traceability monitoring, summary generation, and governance reporting), checks star ratings for data quality, routes findings by compliance status (critical/routine), and produces standardized reports with traceability records. Organizations achieve 90% reduction in reporting cycle time, ensure multi-framework compliance, eliminate manual data aggregation errors, and maintain complete audit trails for regulatory scrutiny.

## Quality Rating Assessment
**What**: Checks ESG data completeness and reliability scores against established quality thresholds  
**Why**: Quality-based filtering identifies data gaps requiring remediation before regulatory submission deadlines

## Intelligent Report Generation
**What**: Routes by status—critical issues trigger immediate alerts, routine generates standard reports with traceability  
**Why**: Risk-based workflows ensure compliance violations receive urgent attention while automating routine disclosures

user.name: Cheng Siong Chin
user.bio: Dr. Cheng Siong CHIN is an n8n workflow creator specializing in AI-powered automation, agent orchestration, and intelligent system integrations. He designs and builds end-to-end workflows that combine LLMs, APIs, and data pipelines to streamline complex processes and deliver production-ready automation solutions. Contact me to discuss custom AI workflows and agent architectures.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
